fields.name: Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the entry, as assigned from the specified number series when the entry was created.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's or customer's trade type to link transactions made for this business partner with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: Gen. Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item's product type to link transactions made for this item with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT entry's posting date.

fields.name: Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the document number on the VAT entry.

fields.name: Document Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the document type that the VAT entry belongs to.

fields.name: Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of the VAT entry.

fields.name: Base
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount that the VAT amount (the amount shown in the Amount field) is calculated from.

fields.name: Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the VAT entry in LCY.

fields.name: VAT Calculation Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies how VAT will be calculated for purchases or sales of items with this particular combination of VAT business posting group and VAT product posting group.

fields.name: Bill-to/Pay-to No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the bill-to customer or pay-to vendor that the entry is linked to.

fields.name: EU 3-Party Trade
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the transaction is related to trade with a third party within the EU.

fields.name: User ID
fields.tooltip: Contains the ID of the user that is associated with the entry.

fields.name: Source Code
fields.tooltip:
Displays the source code that is linked to the VAT entry.
The field can be filled in from the following fields:
If the entry was posted from a journal line, the code is copied from the Source Code field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from an order, invoice or credit memo, the code is retrieved from the Purchases or Sales field in the Source Code Setup table.
If the entry resulted from the Calc. and Post VAT Settlement batch job, the code is retrieved from the VAT Settlement field in the Source Code Setup table.

fields.name: Reason Code
fields.tooltip:
Displays the reason code on the entry.
The field can be filled in from the following fields:
If the entry was posted from a journal line, the code is copied from the Reason Code field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from an order, invoice or credit memo, the code is copied from the Reason Code field on the sales or purchase header.

fields.name: Closed by Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the VAT entry that has closed the entry, if the VAT entry was closed with the Calc. and Post VAT Settlement batch job.

fields.name: Closed
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the VAT entry has been closed by the Calc. and Post VAT Settlement batch job.

fields.name: Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country/region of the address.

fields.name: Internal Ref. No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the internal reference number for the line.

fields.name: Transaction No.
fields.tooltip:
Displays the transaction number assigned to the entry.
The number is used to connect all entries that are created by the same posting.

fields.name: Unrealized Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the unrealized VAT amount for this line if you use unrealized VAT.

fields.name: Unrealized Base
fields.tooltip: Specifies the unrealized base amount if you use unrealized VAT.

fields.name: Remaining Unrealized Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount that remains unrealized in the VAT entry.

fields.name: Remaining Unrealized Base
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of base that remains unrealized in the VAT entry.

fields.name: External Document No.
fields.tooltip:
Displays the external document number for this entry.
The program fills in the field in one of the following ways:
If the entry was posted from a journal line, the number is copied from the External Document No. field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from a sales or purchase order, invoice or credit memo, the number can be copied from the Vendor Invoice No. or the Vendor Cr. Memo No. field on the purchase header or the External Document No. on the sales header, depending on the document type from which the ledger entry was posted.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip: Contains the code for the number series to which the document number on this entry belongs.

fields.name: Tax Area Code
fields.tooltip:
Displays the tax area code that was used when the entry was posted.
The program copied the code from the following fields:
If the entry was posted from a journal line, the code is copied from the Tax Area Code field or the Bal. Tax Area Code field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from an order, invoice or credit memo, the code is copied from the Tax Area Code field on the sales or purchase header.

fields.name: Tax Liable
fields.tooltip: Specifies the value that was copied either from the Tax Liable field, or the Bal. Tax Liable field on a journal line, or the Tax Liable field on the sales or purchase header.

fields.name: Tax Group Code
fields.tooltip:
Displays the tax group code that was used when the entry was posted.
The program automatically retrieves the code from one of the following fields:
If the entry was posted from a journal line, the code is copied from the Tax Group Code field or the Bal. Tax Group Code field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from an order, invoice or credit memo, the code is copied from the Tax Group Code field on the sales or purchase line.

fields.name: Use Tax
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code that is copied from one of the following fields:
If the entry was posted from a journal line, the code is copied from the Use Tax field or the Bal. Use Tax field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from a purchase order, invoice or credit memo, the code was copied from the Use Tax field on the purchase line.

fields.name: Tax Jurisdiction Code
fields.tooltip: Displays the tax jurisdiction that determined the tax accounts and the tax details the program used to calculate the tax amount.

fields.name: Tax Group Used
fields.tooltip: Displays the tax group code from the tax detail the program used to calculate the tax amount.

fields.name: Tax Type
fields.tooltip: Shows whether the entry is to be treated as sales tax or excise tax.

fields.name: Tax on Tax
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether to calculate the sales tax amount with the tax on tax principle. This means that when calculating sales tax for the current jurisdiction, the sales tax amounts for previous jurisdictions are part of the basis.
The order in which tax is calculated for the individual jurisdictions is determined by the setting in the Calculation Order field on the tax area lines.
Example:
If sales tax must be calculated for three jurisdictions at 3%, 5% and 2% respectively and the tax on tax principle is used for the first two jurisdictions, the accumulated sales tax percentage will be:


Sales tax


=


Accumulated sales tax


1 * 0.03

=

0.03000


+ (1 + 0.03) * 0.05

=

0.05150


+ (1 + 0.03 + 0.0515) * 0.02

=

0.02163


Total

=

0.10313 (or 10.313%)

fields.name: Sales Tax Connection No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the link between certain VAT entries.

fields.name: Unrealized VAT Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the original VAT entry, with the unrealized VAT amount, if an unrealized VAT amount is transferred to the realized VAT account.

fields.name: VAT Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT specification of the involved customer or vendor to link transactions made for this record with the appropriate general ledger account according to the VAT posting setup.

fields.name: VAT Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT specification of the involved item or resource to link transactions made for this record with the appropriate general ledger account according to the VAT posting setup.

fields.name: Additional-Currency Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the VAT entry. The amount is in the additional reporting currency.

fields.name: Additional-Currency Base
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount that the VAT amount is calculated from if you post in an additional reporting currency.

fields.name: Add.-Currency Unrealized Amt.
fields.tooltip:
Contains the unrealized VAT amount if you post in an additional reporting currency and use the unrealized VAT features.
The amount in this field is in the additional reporting currency. The program calculates this amount when the transaction is posted.

fields.name: Add.-Currency Unrealized Base
fields.tooltip: Contains the unrealized base VAT amount if you post in an additional reporting currency and use the unrealized VAT features. This amount is in the additional reporting currency, and the program uses this amount to calculate the unrealized VAT amount in the additional reporting currency.

fields.name: VAT Base Discount %
fields.tooltip:
Indicates the percentage that was used for discounting the VAT when a payment was posted.
If the entry was posted from an order, invoice or credit memo, the code is copied from the VAT Base Discount % field on the sales or purchase header.

fields.name: Add.-Curr. Rem. Unreal. Amount
fields.tooltip: Displays the remaining unrealized VAT amount in additional currency for this line if you use the feature unrealized VAT.

fields.name: Add.-Curr. Rem. Unreal. Base
fields.tooltip: Displays the remaining unrealized VAT base amount in additional currency for this line if you use the feature unrealized VAT.

fields.name: VAT Difference
fields.tooltip: Specifies the difference between the calculated VAT amount and a VAT amount that you have entered manually.

fields.name: Add.-Curr. VAT Difference
fields.tooltip: Specifies, in the additional reporting currency, the VAT difference that arises when you make a correction to a VAT amount on a sales or purchase document.

fields.name: Ship-to/Order Address Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the address code of the ship-to customer or order-from vendor that the entry is linked to.

fields.name: Document Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the related document was created.

fields.name: VAT Registration No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT registration number of the customer or vendor that the entry is linked to.

fields.name: Reversed
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the entry has been part of a reverse transaction.

fields.name: Reversed by Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the correcting entry. If the field Specifies a number, the entry cannot be reversed again.

fields.name: Reversed Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the original entry that was undone by the reverse transaction.

fields.name: EU Service
fields.tooltip: Specifies if this VAT entry is to be reported as a service in the periodic VAT reports.

fields.name: VAT Reporting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT date on the VAT entry. This is either the date that the document was created or posted, depending on your setting on the General Ledger Setup page.

fields.name: Non-Deductible VAT Base
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of VAT that is not deducted due to the type of goods or services purchased.

fields.name: Non-Deductible VAT Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the transaction for which VAT is not applied, due to the type of goods or services purchased.

fields.name: Non-Deductible VAT Base ACY
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of VAT that is not deducted due to the type of goods or services purchased. The amount is in the additional reporting currency.

fields.name: Non-Deductible VAT Amount ACY
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the transaction for which VAT is not applied, due to the type of goods or services purchased. The amount is in the additional reporting currency.

fields.name: Non-Deductible VAT Diff.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the difference between the calculated Non-Deductible VAT amount and a Non-Deductible VAT amount that you have entered manually.
